message:
refactor(pager): preserve fast-array backing storage on cache.clear() + rustfmt cleanup

ShardedPageCache::clear() now invokes FastArray::clear() instead of
cold_reset(). The behavioral difference is subtle but important: clear()
preserves the underlying Vec allocation, so a workload that sparsely
populates the fast-array, flushes the cache, and then re-populates the
same sparse working set avoids re-growing the backing storage on every
cycle.

Previously cold_reset() shrank the backing storage to FAST_ARRAY_INITIAL_CAPACITY
on every clear(), forcing the next sparse insert to re-allocate. The
inverted unit test (renamed from test_sharded_cache_clear_releases_sparse_fast_path_storage
to test_sharded_cache_clear_preserves_sparse_fast_path_storage) now asserts
the new invariant: pages.len() after clear() equals pages.len() before clear().

Also captures a snapshot of resident_pages() before iterating to avoid
holding the fast-array lock across the per-shard distribution counter
update, which previously had a small re-entrance risk.

The other two files (connection.rs, pager.rs) carry only rustfmt-driven
formatting changes (line-wrap normalization on long match arms,
inner.freelist.retain(...) one-liners). No semantics changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
